name: Gogo Inc.
business: Gogo Inc. is the only multi-orbit, multi-band in-flight connectivity provider offering connectivity technology purpose-built for business and military/government aviation. The company provides broadband connectivity services through air-to-ground technology and integrated low earth orbit and geostationary earth orbit satellite solutions, as well as equipment sales.

exposures.sentence: Gogo is the only multi-orbit, multi-band in-flight connectivity provider offering connectivity technology purpose-built for business and military/government aviation.

We also serve our growing military/government customer base by providing cost-effective, turnkey in-flight connectivity that integrates innovative, commercially proven technologies and software to deliver mission-tailored capabilities.
The infrastructure supporting our in-flight connectivity services consists of our networks, towers, cybersecurity software, and data centers.
We continue to augment our ATG broadband connectivity services through the addition of our fourth ATG broadband network (Gogo 5G), which we launched in the fourth quarter of 2025.

catalysts.sentence: We continue to augment our ATG broadband connectivity services through the addition of our fourth ATG broadband network (Gogo 5G), which we launched in the fourth quarter of 2025.
We commercially launched the first global LEO broadband satellite service purpose-built for business aviation (Gogo Galileo) in the first quarter of 2025.
Renewal applications for both ATG licenses must be filed by October 31, 2026.
On May 8, 2025, the FCC established a new program-wide completion deadline of May 8, 2026, for all Priority 1 participants, which includes the Company.

catalysts.event: LTE network transition for subset of customers
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate this subset of customers will see improved performance because of this network transition, which is expected to occur in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: As of December 31, 2025, our market was comprised of approximately 27,000 business aircraft in North America, of which approximately 30% have broadband connectivity, and approximately 15,000 business aircraft in the rest of the world, of which approximately 7% have broadband connectivity.
tamClaims.sentence: As of December 31, 2025, our market was comprised of approximately 27,000 business aircraft in North America, of which approximately 30% have broadband connectivity, and approximately 15,000 business aircraft in the rest of the world, of which approximately 7% have broadband connectivity.
